17	package networking
18	
19	import (
20		"errors"
21		"fmt"
22		"os"
23		"path/filepath"
24	
25		"golang.org/x/sys/unix"
26	
27		"k8s.io/kops/nodeup/pkg/model"
28		"k8s.io/kops/pkg/apis/kops"
29		"k8s.io/kops/upup/pkg/fi"
30		"k8s.io/kops/upup/pkg/fi/nodeup/nodetasks"
31	)
32	
33	// CiliumBuilder writes Cilium's assets
34	type CiliumBuilder struct {
35		*model.NodeupModelContext
36	}
37	
38	var _ fi.NodeupModelBuilder = &CiliumBuilder{}
39	
40	// Build is responsible for configuring the network cni
41	func (b *CiliumBuilder) Build(c *fi.NodeupModelBuilderContext) error {
42		// As long as the Cilium Etcd cluster exists, we should do this
43		if b.NodeupConfig.UseCiliumEtcd {
44			if err := b.buildCiliumEtcdSecrets(c); err != nil {
45				return err
46			}
47		}
48	
49		if b.NodeupConfig.Networking.Cilium == nil {
50			return nil
51		}
52	
53		if err := b.buildBPFMount(c); err != nil {
54			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create bpf mount unit: %w", err)
55		}
56	
57		if err := b.buildCgroup2Mount(c); err != nil {
58			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create cgroupv2 mount unit: %w", err)
59		}
60	
61		disableManageForeignRoutes(c, b.Distribution)
62		disableCloudInitNetworkHotplug(c, b.Distribution)
63	
64		if b.NodeupConfig.Networking.Cilium.IPAM == kops.CiliumIpamEni {
65			maskEC2NetUtilsUdevRules(c, b.Distribution)
66			setMACAddressPolicyNone(c, b.Distribution)
67			markSecondaryENIsUnmanaged(c, b.Distribution)
68		}
69	
70		return nil
71	}
72	
73	func (b *CiliumBuilder) buildBPFMount(c *fi.NodeupModelBuilderContext) error {
74		var fsdata unix.Statfs_t
75		err := unix.Statfs("/sys/fs/bpf", &fsdata)
76		if err != nil {
77			return fmt.Errorf("error checking for /sys/fs/bpf: %v", err)
78		}
79	
80		// equivalent to unix.BPF_FS_MAGIC in golang.org/x/sys/unix
81		BPF_FS_MAGIC := uint32(0xcafe4a11)
82	
83		// systemd v238 includes the bpffs mount by default; and gives an error "has a bad unit file setting" if we try to mount it again (see mount_point_is_api)
84		alreadyMounted := uint32(fsdata.Type) == BPF_FS_MAGIC
85	
86		if !alreadyMounted {
87			unit := `
88	[Unit]
89	Description=Cilium BPF mounts
90	Documentation=http://docs.cilium.io/
91	DefaultDependencies=no
92	Before=local-fs.target umount.target kubelet.service
93	
94	[Mount]
95	What=bpffs
96	Where=/sys/fs/bpf
97	Type=bpf
98	
99	[Install]
100	WantedBy=multi-user.target
101	`
102	
103			service := &nodetasks.Service{
104				Name:       "sys-fs-bpf.mount",
105				Definition: fi.PtrTo(unit),
106			}
107			service.InitDefaults()
108			c.AddTask(service)
109		}
110	
111		return nil
112	}
113	
114	func (b *CiliumBuilder) buildCgroup2Mount(c *fi.NodeupModelBuilderContext) error {
115		cgroupPath := "/run/cilium/cgroupv2"
116	
117		var fsdata unix.Statfs_t
118		err := unix.Statfs(cgroupPath, &fsdata)
119	
120		// If the path does not exist, systemd will create it
121		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
122			return fmt.Errorf("error checking for /run/cilium/cgroupv2: %v", err)
123		}
124	
125		CGROUP_FS_MAGIC := uint32(0x63677270)
126	
127		alreadyMounted := uint32(fsdata.Type) == CGROUP_FS_MAGIC
128	
129		if !alreadyMounted {
130			unit := `
131	[Unit]
132	Description=Cilium Cgroup2 mounts
133	Documentation=http://docs.cilium.io/
134	DefaultDependencies=no
135	Before=local-fs.target umount.target kubelet.service
136	
137	[Mount]
138	What=cgroup2
139	Where=/run/cilium/cgroupv2
140	Type=cgroup2
141	
142	[Install]
143	WantedBy=multi-user.target
144	`
145	
146			service := &nodetasks.Service{
147				Name:         "run-cilium-cgroupv2.mount",
148				Definition:   fi.PtrTo(unit),
149				SmartRestart: fi.PtrTo(false),
150			}
151			service.InitDefaults()
152			c.AddTask(service)
153		}
154	
155		return nil
156	}
157	
158	func (b *CiliumBuilder) buildCiliumEtcdSecrets(c *fi.NodeupModelBuilderContext) error {
159		name := "etcd-client-cilium"
160		dir := "/etc/kubernetes/pki/cilium"
161		signer := "etcd-clients-ca-cilium"
162		c.AddTask(&nodetasks.File{
163			Path:     filepath.Join(dir, "etcd-ca.crt"),
164			Contents: fi.NewStringResource(b.NodeupConfig.CAs[signer]),
165			Type:     nodetasks.FileType_File,
166			Mode:     fi.PtrTo("0600"),
167		})
168		if b.HasAPIServer {
169			issueCert := &nodetasks.IssueCert{
170				Name:      name,
171				Signer:    signer,
172				KeypairID: b.NodeupConfig.KeypairIDs[signer],
173				Type:      "client",
174				Subject: nodetasks.PKIXName{
175					CommonName: "cilium",
176				},
177			}
178			c.AddTask(issueCert)
179			return issueCert.AddFileTasks(c, dir, name, "", nil)
180		} else {
181			cert, key, err := b.GetBootstrapCert(name, signer)
182			if err != nil {
183				return err
184			}
185	
186			c.AddTask(&nodetasks.File{
187				Path:           filepath.Join(dir, name+".crt"),
188				Contents:       cert,
189				Type:           nodetasks.FileType_File,
190				Mode:           fi.PtrTo("0644"),
191				BeforeServices: []string{"kubelet.service"},
192			})
193	
194			c.AddTask(&nodetasks.File{
195				Path:           filepath.Join(dir, name+".key"),
196				Contents:       key,
197				Type:           nodetasks.FileType_File,
198				Mode:           fi.PtrTo("0400"),
199				BeforeServices: []string{"kubelet.service"},
200			})

202			return nil
203		}

17	package bootstrapchannelbuilder
18	
19	import (
20		"k8s.io/klog/v2"
21		"k8s.io/kops/channels/pkg/api"
22		"k8s.io/kops/upup/pkg/fi"
23	)
24	
25	func addCiliumAddon(b *BootstrapChannelBuilder, addons *AddonList) error {
26		cilium := b.Cluster.Spec.Networking.Cilium
27		if cilium == nil {
28			return nil
29		}
30	
31		key := "networking.cilium.io"
32		useBuiltin := !b.hasExternalAddon(key)
33	
34		if !useBuiltin {
35			klog.Infof("found cilium (%q) in addons; won't use builtin", key)
36		} else {
37			id := "k8s-1.16"
38			location := key + "/" + id + "-v1.15.yaml"
39	
40			addon := &api.AddonSpec{
41				Name:               fi.PtrTo(key),
42				Selector:           networkingSelector(),
43				Manifest:           fi.PtrTo(location),
44				Id:                 id,
45				NeedsRollingUpdate: api.NeedsRollingUpdateAll,
46			}
47			if cilium.Hubble != nil && fi.ValueOf(cilium.Hubble.Enabled) {
48				addon.NeedsPKI = true
49			}
50			addons.Add(addon)
51		}
52		return nil
53	}

content.content:
57	# Source: cilium/templates/cilium-configmap.yaml
58	apiVersion: v1
59	kind: ConfigMap
60	metadata:
61	  name: cilium-config
62	  namespace: kube-system
63	data:
64	  {{ if .EtcdManaged }}
65	  # The kvstore configuration is used to enable use of a kvstore for state
66	  # storage. This can be provided with an external kvstore.
67	  kvstore: etcd
68	  kvstore-opt: '{"etcd.config": "/var/lib/etcd-config/etcd.config"}'
69	
70	  # This etcd-config contains the etcd endpoints of your cluster. If you use
71	  # TLS please make sure you follow the tutorial in https://cilium.link/etcd-config
72	  etcd-config: |-
73	    ---
74	    endpoints:
75	      - https://{{ APIInternalName }}:4003
76	    trusted-ca-file: '/var/lib/etcd-secrets/etcd-ca.crt'
77	    key-file: '/var/lib/etcd-secrets/etcd-client-cilium.key'
78	    cert-file: '/var/lib/etcd-secrets/etcd-client-cilium.crt'
79	  {{ end }}
80	
81	  # Identity allocation mode selects how identities are shared between cilium
82	  # nodes by setting how they are stored. The options are "crd", "kvstore" or
83	  # "doublewrite-readkvstore" / "doublewrite-readcrd".
84	  # - "crd" stores identities in kubernetes as CRDs (custom resource definition).
85	  #   These can be queried with:
86	  #     kubectl get ciliumid
87	  # - "kvstore" stores identities in an etcd kvstore, that is
88	  #   configured below. Cilium versions before 1.6 supported only the kvstore
89	  #   backend. Upgrades from these older cilium versions should continue using
90	  #   the kvstore by commenting out the identity-allocation-mode below, or
91	  #   setting it to "kvstore".
92	  # - "doublewrite" modes store identities in both the kvstore and CRDs. This is useful
93	  #   for seamless migrations from the kvstore mode to the crd mode. Consult the
94	  #   documentation for more information on how to perform the migration.
95	  identity-allocation-mode: "{{ .IdentityAllocationMode }}"
96	
97	  identity-heartbeat-timeout: "30m0s"
98	  identity-gc-interval: "15m0s"
99	  cilium-endpoint-gc-interval: "5m0s"
100	  nodes-gc-interval: "5m0s"
101	  # Disable the usage of CiliumEndpoint CRD
102	  disable-endpoint-crd: "{{ .DisableEndpointCRD }}"
103	  # identity-change-grace-period is the grace period that needs to pass
104	  # before an endpoint that has changed its identity will start using
105	  # that new identity. During the grace period, the new identity has
106	  # already been allocated and other nodes in the cluster have a chance
107	  # to whitelist the new upcoming identity of the endpoint.
108	  identity-change-grace-period: "{{ .IdentityChangeGracePeriod }}"
109	
110	  # If you want to run cilium in debug mode change this value to true
111	  debug: "{{ .Debug }}"
112	  debug-verbose: ""
113	  metrics-sampling-interval: "5m"
114	  # The agent can be put into the following three policy enforcement modes
115	  # default, always and never.
116	  # https://docs.cilium.io/en/latest/security/policy/intro/#policy-enforcement-modes
117	  enable-policy: "default"
118	  policy-cidr-match-mode: ""
119	  {{ if .EnablePrometheusMetrics }}
120	  # If you want metrics enabled in all of your Cilium agents, set the port for
121	  # which the Cilium agents will have their metrics exposed.
122	  # This option deprecates the "prometheus-serve-addr" in the
123	  # "cilium-metrics-config" ConfigMap
124	  # NOTE that this will open the port on ALL nodes where Cilium pods are
125	  # scheduled.
126	  prometheus-serve-addr: ":{{ .AgentPrometheusPort }}"
127	  {{ if .Metrics }}
128	  # Metrics that should be enabled or disabled from the default metric
129	  # list. (+metric_foo to enable metric_foo , -metric_bar to disable
130	  # metric_bar).
131	  metrics: {{- range .Metrics }}
132	    {{ . }}
133	  {{- end }}
134	  {{ end }}
135	  # A space-separated list of controller groups for which to enable metrics.
136	  # The special values of "all" and "none" are supported.
137	  controller-group-metrics:
138	    write-cni-file
139	    sync-host-ips
140	    sync-lb-maps-with-k8s-services
141	  # Port to expose Envoy metrics (e.g. "9964"). Envoy metrics listener will be disabled if this
142	  # field is not set.
143	  proxy-prometheus-port: "9964"
144	  # If you want metrics enabled in cilium-operator, set the port for
145	  # which the Cilium Operator will have their metrics exposed.
146	  # NOTE that this will open the port on the nodes where Cilium operator pod
147	  # is scheduled.
148	  operator-prometheus-serve-addr: ":9963"
149	  enable-metrics: "true"
150	  {{ end }}
151	
152	  {{ if or (WithDefaultBool .GatewayAPI.Enabled false) (WithDefaultBool .Ingress.Enabled false) }}
153	  enable-envoy-config: "true"
154	  envoy-config-retry-interval: "15s"
155	  envoy-secrets-namespace: "kube-system"
156	  {{ end }}
157	  {{ if WithDefaultBool .Ingress.Enabled false }}
158	  enable-ingress-controller: "true"
159	  {{ if .Ingress.EnforceHttps }}
160	  enforce-ingress-https: "{{ .Ingress.EnforceHttps }}"
161	  {{ end }}
162	  enable-ingress-proxy-protocol: "false"
163	  {{ if .Ingress.EnableSecretsSync }}
164	  enable-ingress-secrets-sync: "{{ .Ingress.EnableSecretsSync }}"
165	  {{ end }}
166	  ingress-secrets-namespace: "kube-system"
167	  {{ if .Ingress.LoadBalancerAnnotationPrefixes }}
168	  ingress-lb-annotation-prefixes: "{{ .Ingress.LoadBalancerAnnotationPrefixes }}"
169	  {{ end }}
170	  {{ if .Ingress.DefaultLoadBalancerMode }}
171	  ingress-default-lb-mode: "{{ .Ingress.DefaultLoadBalancerMode }}"
172	  {{ end }}
173	  {{ if .Ingress.SharedLoadBalancerServiceName }}
174	  ingress-shared-lb-service-name: "{{ .Ingress.SharedLoadBalancerServiceName }}"
175	  {{ end }}
176	  ingress-hostnetwork-enabled: "false"
177	  ingress-hostnetwork-shared-listener-port: "8080"
178	  ingress-hostnetwork-nodelabelselector: ""
179	  {{ end }}
180	
181	  {{ if WithDefaultBool .GatewayAPI.Enabled false }}
182	  enable-gateway-api: "true"
183	  {{ if .GatewayAPI.EnableSecretsSync }}
184	  enable-gateway-api-secrets-sync: "{{ .GatewayAPI.EnableSecretsSync }}"
185	  {{ end }}
186	  enable-gateway-api-proxy-protocol: "false"
187	  enable-gateway-api-app-protocol: "false"
188	  enable-gateway-api-alpn: "false"
189	  gateway-api-xff-num-trusted-hops: "0"
190	  gateway-api-service-externaltrafficpolicy: "Cluster"
191	  gateway-api-secrets-namespace: "kube-system"
192	  gateway-api-hostnetwork-enabled: "false"
193	  gateway-api-hostnetwork-nodelabelselector: ""
194	  {{ end }}
195	
196	  # Enable IPv4 addressing. If enabled, all endpoints are allocated an IPv4
197	  # address.
198	  enable-ipv4: "{{ not IsIPv6Only }}"
199	
200	  # Enable IPv6 addressing. If enabled, all endpoints are allocated an IPv6
201	  # address.
202	  enable-ipv6: "{{ IsIPv6Only }}"
203	  # Users who wish to specify their own custom CNI configuration file must set
204	  # custom-cni-conf to "true", otherwise Cilium may overwrite the configuration.
205	  custom-cni-conf: "false"
206	  enable-bpf-clock-probe: "false"
207	  # If you want cilium monitor to aggregate tracing for packets, set this level
208	  # to "low", "medium", or "maximum". The higher the level, the less packets
209	  # that will be seen in monitor output.
210	  monitor-aggregation: "{{ .MonitorAggregation }}"
211	
212	  # The monitor aggregation interval governs the typical time between monitor
213	  # notification events for each allowed connection.
214	  #
215	  # Only effective when monitor aggregation is set to "medium" or higher.
216	  monitor-aggregation-interval: "5s"
217	
218	  # The monitor aggregation flags determine which TCP flags which, upon the
219	  # first observation, cause monitor notifications to be generated.
220	  #
221	  # Only effective when monitor aggregation is set to "medium" or higher.
222	  monitor-aggregation-flags: all
223	  # Specifies the ratio (0.0-1.0] of total system memory to use for dynamic
224	  # sizing of the TCP CT, non-TCP CT, NAT and policy BPF maps.
225	  bpf-map-dynamic-size-ratio: "0.0025"
226	  {{ if .ChainingMode }}
227	  # In cni chaining mode, the other chained plugin is responsible for underlying connectivity,
228	  # so cilium eBPF host routing shoud not work, and let it fall back to the legacy routing mode
229	  enable-host-legacy-routing: "true"
230	  {{ end }}
231	  # bpf-policy-map-max specifies the maximum number of entries in endpoint
232	  # policy map (per endpoint)
233	  bpf-policy-map-max: "16384"
234	  # bpf-policy-stats-map-max specifies the maximum number of entries in global
235	  # policy stats map
236	  bpf-policy-stats-map-max: "65536"
237	  # bpf-lb-map-max specifies the maximum number of entries in bpf lb service,
238	  # backend and affinity maps.
239	  bpf-lb-map-max: "65536"
240	  bpf-lb-external-clusterip: "false"
241	  bpf-lb-source-range-all-types: "false"
242	  bpf-lb-algorithm-annotation: "false"
243	  bpf-lb-mode-annotation: "false"
244	
245	  bpf-distributed-lru: "false"
246	  bpf-events-drop-enabled: "true"
247	  bpf-events-policy-verdict-enabled: "true"
248	  bpf-events-trace-enabled: "true"
249	
250	  # Pre-allocation of map entries allows per-packet latency to be reduced, at
251	  # the expense of up-front memory allocation for the entries in the maps. The
252	  # default value below will minimize memory usage in the default installation;
253	  # users who are sensitive to latency may consider setting this to "true".
254	  #
255	  # This option was introduced in Cilium 1.4. Cilium 1.3 and earlier ignore
256	  # this option and behave as though it is set to "true".
257	  #
258	  # If this value is modified, then during the next Cilium startup the restore
259	  # of existing endpoints and tracking of ongoing connections may be disrupted.
260	  # As a result, reply packets may be dropped and the load-balancing decisions
261	  # for established connections may change.
262	  #
263	  # If this option is set to "false" during an upgrade from 1.3 or earlier to
264	  # 1.4 or later, then it may cause one-time disruptions during the upgrade.
265	  preallocate-bpf-maps: "{{- if .PreallocateBPFMaps -}}true{{- else -}}false{{- end -}}"
266	
267	  # Name of the cluster. Only relevant when building a mesh of clusters.
268	  cluster-name: "{{ .ClusterName }}"
269	  # Unique ID of the cluster. Must be unique across all conneted clusters and
270	  # in the range of 1 and 255. Only relevant when building a mesh of clusters.
271	  {{ if .ClusterID }}
272	  cluster-id: "{{ .ClusterID }}"
273	  {{ end }}
274	
275	  # Encapsulation mode for communication between nodes
276	  # Possible values:
277	  #   - disabled
278	  #   - vxlan (default)
279	  #   - geneve
280	
281	  {{ if eq .Tunnel "disabled" }}
282	  # This option enables native-routing mode, in place of tunnel=disabled, now deprecated.
283	  routing-mode: "native"
284	  {{ else }}
285	  routing-mode: "tunnel"
286	  tunnel-protocol: "{{ .Tunnel }}"
287	  tunnel-source-port-range: "0-0"
288	  service-no-backend-response: "reject"
289	  {{ end }}
290	
291	
292	  # Enables L7 proxy for L7 policy enforcement and visibility
293	  enable-l7-proxy: "{{ .EnableL7Proxy }}"
294	
295	  {{ if .ChainingMode }}
296	  # Enable chaining with another CNI plugin
297	  #
298	  # Supported modes:
299	  #  - none
300	  #  - aws-cni
301	  #  - flannel
302	  #  - generic-veth
303	  #  - portmap (Enables HostPort support for Cilium)
304	  cni-chaining-mode: "{{ .ChainingMode }}"
305	  {{ if ne .ChainingMode "portmap" }}
306	  # Disable the PodCIDR route to the cilium_host interface as it is not
307	  # required. While chaining, it is the responsibility of the underlying plugin
308	  # to enable routing.
309	  enable-local-node-route: "false"
310	  {{ end }}
311	  {{ end }}
312	
313	  enable-ipv4-masquerade: "{{ .Masquerade }}"
314	  enable-ipv4-big-tcp: "false"
315	  enable-ipv6-big-tcp: "false"
316	  enable-ipv6-masquerade: "false"

Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# Source: cilium/templates/cilium-configmap.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: cilium-config
  namespace: kube-system
data:
  {{ if .EtcdManaged }}
  # The kvstore configuration is used to enable use of a kvstore for state
  # storage. This can be provided with an external kvstore.
  kvstore: etcd
  kvstore-opt: '{"etcd.config": "/var/lib/etcd-config/etcd.config"}'

  # This etcd-config contains the etcd endpoints of your cluster. If you use
  # TLS please make sure you follow the tutorial in https://cilium.link/etcd-config
  etcd-config: |-
    ---
    endpoints:
      - https://{{ APIInternalName }}:4003
    trusted-ca-file: '/var/lib/etcd-secrets/etcd-ca.crt'
    key-file: '/var/lib/etcd-secrets/etcd-client-cilium.key'
    cert-file: '/var/lib/etcd-secrets/etcd-client-cilium.crt'
  {{ end }}

  # Identity allocation mode selects how identities are shared between cilium
  # nodes by setting how they are stored. The options are "crd", "kvstore" or
  # "doublewrite-readkvstore" / "doublewrite-readcrd".
  # - "crd" stores identities in kubernetes as CRDs (custom resource definition).
  #   These can be queried with:
  #     kubectl get ciliumid
  # - "kvstore" stores identities in an etcd kvstore, that is
  #   configured below. Cilium versions before 1.6 supported only the kvstore
  #   backend. Upgrades from these older cilium versions should continue using
  #   the kvstore by commenting out the identity-allocation-mode below, or
  #   setting it to "kvstore".
  # - "doublewrite" modes store identities in both the kvstore and CRDs. This is useful
  #   for seamless migrations from the kvstore mode to the crd mode. Consult the
  #   documentation for more information on how to perform the migration.
  identity-allocation-mode: "{{ .IdentityAllocationMode }}"

  identity-heartbeat-timeout: "30m0s"
  identity-gc-interval: "15m0s"
  cilium-endpoint-gc-interval: "5m0s"
  nodes-gc-interval: "5m0s"
  # Disable the usage of CiliumEndpoint CRD
  disable-endpoint-crd: "{{ .DisableEndpointCRD }}"
  # identity-change-grace-period is the grace period that needs to pass
  # before an endpoint that has changed its identity will start using
  # that new identity. During the grace period, the new identity has
  # already been allocated and other nodes in the cluster have a chance
  # to whitelist the new upcoming identity of the endpoint.
  identity-change-grace-period: "{{ .IdentityChangeGracePeriod }}"

  # If you want to run cilium in debug mode change this value to true
  debug: "{{ .Debug }}"
  debug-verbose: ""
  metrics-sampling-interval: "5m"
  # The agent can be put into the following three policy enforcement modes
  # default, always and never.
  # https://docs.cilium.io/en/latest/security/policy/intro/#policy-enforcement-modes
  enable-policy: "default"
  policy-cidr-match-mode: ""
  {{ if .EnablePrometheusMetrics }}
  # If you want metrics enabled in all of your Cilium agents, set the port for
  # which the Cilium agents will have their metrics exposed.
  # This option deprecates the "prometheus-serve-addr" in the
  # "cilium-metrics-config" ConfigMap
  # NOTE that this will open the port on ALL nodes where Cilium pods are
  # scheduled.
  prometheus-serve-addr: ":{{ .AgentPrometheusPort }}"
  {{ if .Metrics }}
  # Metrics that should be enabled or disabled from the default metric
  # list. (+metric_foo to enable metric_foo , -metric_bar to disable
  # metric_bar).
  metrics: {{- range .Metrics }}
    {{ . }}
  {{- end }}
  {{ end }}
  # A space-separated list of controller groups for which to enable metrics.
  # The special values of "all" and "none" are supported.
  controller-group-metrics:
    write-cni-file
    sync-host-ips
    sync-lb-maps-with-k8s-services
  # Port to expose Envoy metrics (e.g. "9964"). Envoy metrics listener will be disabled if this
  # field is not set.
  proxy-prometheus-port: "9964"
  # If you want metrics enabled in cilium-operator, set the port for
  # which the Cilium Operator will have their metrics exposed.
  # NOTE that this will open the port on the nodes where Cilium operator pod
  # is scheduled.
  operator-prometheus-serve-addr: ":9963"
  enable-metrics: "true"
  {{ end }}

  {{ if or (WithDefaultBool .GatewayAPI.Enabled false) (WithDefaultBool .Ingress.Enabled false) }}
  enable-envoy-config: "true"
  envoy-config-retry-interval: "15s"
  envoy-secrets-namespace: "kube-system"
  {{ end }}
  {{ if WithDefaultBool .Ingress.Enabled false }}
  enable-ingress-controller: "true"
  {{ if .Ingress.EnforceHttps }}
  enforce-ingress-https: "{{ .Ingress.EnforceHttps }}"
  {{ end }}
  enable-ingress-proxy-protocol: "false"
  {{ if .Ingress.EnableSecretsSync }}
  enable-ingress-secrets-sync: "{{ .Ingress.EnableSecretsSync }}"
  {{ end }}
  ingress-secrets-namespace: "kube-system"
  {{ if .Ingress.LoadBalancerAnnotationPrefixes }}
  ingress-lb-annotation-prefixes: "{{ .Ingress.LoadBalancerAnnotationPrefixes }}"
  {{ end }}
  {{ if .Ingress.DefaultLoadBalancerMode }}
  ingress-default-lb-mode: "{{ .Ingress.DefaultLoadBalancerMode }}"
  {{ end }}
  {{ if .Ingress.SharedLoadBalancerServiceName }}
  ingress-shared-lb-service-name: "{{ .Ingress.SharedLoadBalancerServiceName }}"
  {{ end }}
  ingress-hostnetwork-enabled: "false"
  ingress-hostnetwork-shared-listener-port: "8080"
  ingress-hostnetwork-nodelabelselector: ""
  {{ end }}

  {{ if WithDefaultBool .GatewayAPI.Enabled false }}
  enable-gateway-api: "true"
  {{ if .GatewayAPI.EnableSecretsSync }}
  enable-gateway-api-secrets-sync: "{{ .GatewayAPI.EnableSecretsSync }}"
  {{ end }}
  enable-gateway-api-proxy-protocol: "false"
  enable-gateway-api-app-protocol: "false"
  enable-gateway-api-alpn: "false"
  gateway-api-xff-num-trusted-hops: "0"
  gateway-api-service-externaltrafficpolicy: "Cluster"
  gateway-api-secrets-namespace: "kube-system"
  gateway-api-hostnetwork-enabled: "false"
  gateway-api-hostnetwork-nodelabelselector: ""
  {{ end }}

  # Enable IPv4 addressing. If enabled, all endpoints are allocated an IPv4
  # address.
  enable-ipv4: "{{ not IsIPv6Only }}"

  # Enable IPv6 addressing. If enabled, all endpoints are allocated an IPv6
  # address.
  enable-ipv6: "{{ IsIPv6Only }}"
  # Users who wish to specify their own custom CNI configuration file must set
  # custom-cni-conf to "true", otherwise Cilium may overwrite the configuration.
  custom-cni-conf: "false"
  enable-bpf-clock-probe: "false"
  # If you want cilium monitor to aggregate tracing for packets, set this level
  # to "low", "medium", or "maximum". The higher the level, the less packets
  # that will be seen in monitor output.
  monitor-aggregation: "{{ .MonitorAggregation }}"

  # The monitor aggregation interval governs the typical time between monitor
  # notification events for each allowed connection.
  #
  # Only effective when monitor aggregation is set to "medium" or higher.
  monitor-aggregation-interval: "5s"

  # The monitor aggregation flags determine which TCP flags which, upon the
  # first observation, cause monitor notifications to be generated.
  #
  # Only effective when monitor aggregation is set to "medium" or higher.
  monitor-aggregation-flags: all
  # Specifies the ratio (0.0-1.0] of total system memory to use for dynamic
  # sizing of the TCP CT, non-TCP CT, NAT and policy BPF maps.
  bpf-map-dynamic-size-ratio: "0.0025"
  {{ if .ChainingMode }}
  # In cni chaining mode, the other chained plugin is responsible for underlying connectivity,
  # so cilium eBPF host routing shoud not work, and let it fall back to the legacy routing mode
  enable-host-legacy-routing: "true"
  {{ end }}
  # bpf-policy-map-max specifies the maximum number of entries in endpoint
  # policy map (per endpoint)
  bpf-policy-map-max: "16384"
  # bpf-policy-stats-map-max specifies the maximum number of entries in global
  # policy stats map
  bpf-policy-stats-map-max: "65536"
  # bpf-lb-map-max specifies the maximum number of entries in bpf lb service,
  # backend and affinity maps.
  bpf-lb-map-max: "65536"
  bpf-lb-external-clusterip: "false"
  bpf-lb-source-range-all-types: "false"
  bpf-lb-algorithm-annotation: "false"
  bpf-lb-mode-annotation: "false"

  bpf-distributed-lru: "false"
  bpf-events-drop-enabled: "true"
  bpf-events-policy-verdict-enabled: "true"
  bpf-events-trace-enabled: "true"

  # Pre-allocation of map entries allows per-packet latency to be reduced, at
  # the expense of up-front memory allocation for the entries in the maps. The
  # default value below will minimize memory usage in the default installation;
  # users who are sensitive to latency may consider setting this to "true".
  #
  # This option was introduced in Cilium 1.4. Cilium 1.3 and earlier ignore
  # this option and behave as though it is set to "true".
  #
  # If this value is modified, then during the next Cilium startup the restore
  # of existing endpoints and tracking of ongoing connections may be disrupted.
  # As a result, reply packets may be dropped and the load-balancing decisions
  # for established connections may change.
  #
  # If this option is set to "false" during an upgrade from 1.3 or earlier to
  # 1.4 or later, then it may cause one-time disruptions during the upgrade.
  preallocate-bpf-maps: "{{- if .PreallocateBPFMaps -}}true{{- else -}}false{{- end -}}"

  # Name of the cluster. Only relevant when building a mesh of clusters.
  cluster-name: "{{ .ClusterName }}"
  # Unique ID of the cluster. Must be unique across all conneted clusters and
  # in the range of 1 and 255. Only relevant when building a mesh of clusters.
  {{ if .ClusterID }}
  cluster-id: "{{ .ClusterID }}"
  {{ end }}

  # Encapsulation mode for communication between nodes
  # Possible values:
  #   - disabled
  #   - vxlan (default)
  #   - geneve

  {{ if eq .Tunnel "disabled" }}
  # This option enables native-routing mode, in place of tunnel=disabled, now deprecated.
  routing-mode: "native"
  {{ else }}
  routing-mode: "tunnel"
  tunnel-protocol: "{{ .Tunnel }}"
  tunnel-source-port-range: "0-0"
  service-no-backend-response: "reject"
  {{ end }}


  # Enables L7 proxy for L7 policy enforcement and visibility
  enable-l7-proxy: "{{ .EnableL7Proxy }}"

  {{ if .ChainingMode }}
  # Enable chaining with another CNI plugin
  #
  # Supported modes:
  #  - none
  #  - aws-cni
  #  - flannel
  #  - generic-veth
  #  - portmap (Enables HostPort support for Cilium)
  cni-chaining-mode: "{{ .ChainingMode }}"
  {{ if ne .ChainingMode "portmap" }}
  # Disable the PodCIDR route to the cilium_host interface as it is not
  # required. While chaining, it is the responsibility of the underlying plugin
  # to enable routing.
  enable-local-node-route: "false"
  {{ end }}
  {{ end }}

  enable-ipv4-masquerade: "{{ .Masquerade }}"
  enable-ipv4-big-tcp: "false"
  enable-ipv6-big-tcp: "false"
  enable-ipv6-masquerade: "false"

content.content:
313	  enable-ipv4-masquerade: "{{ .Masquerade }}"
314	  enable-ipv4-big-tcp: "false"
315	  enable-ipv6-big-tcp: "false"
316	  enable-ipv6-masquerade: "false"
317	  enable-tcx: "true"
318	  datapath-mode: "veth"
319	  enable-bpf-masquerade: "{{ and (WithDefaultBool .EnableBPFMasquerade false) (not IsIPv6Only) }}"
320	  enable-masquerade-to-route-source: "false"
321	  {{ if .EnableEncryption }}
322	  {{ if eq .EncryptionType "ipsec" }}
323	  enable-ipsec: "true"
324	  ipsec-key-file: /etc/ipsec/keys
325	  enable-ipsec-key-watcher: "true"
326	  ipsec-key-rotation-duration: "5m"
327	  enable-ipsec-encrypted-overlay: "false"
328	  {{ else if eq .EncryptionType "wireguard"  }}
329	  enable-wireguard: "true"
330	  {{ end }}
331	  encrypt-node: "{{ .NodeEncryption }}"
332	  {{ end }}
333	
334	  enable-xt-socket-fallback: "true"
335	  install-no-conntrack-iptables-rules: "false"
336	  iptables-random-fully: "false"
337	
338	  auto-direct-node-routes: "{{ .AutoDirectNodeRoutes }}"
339	  direct-routing-skip-unreachable: "false"
340	
341	
342	  enable-local-redirect-policy: "{{ .EnableLocalRedirectPolicy }}"
343	
344	  kube-proxy-replacement: "{{- if .EnableNodePort -}}true{{- else -}}false{{- end -}}"
345	  bpf-lb-sock: "false"
346	  enable-node-port: "{{ .EnableNodePort }}"
347	  nodeport-addresses: ""
348	  enable-health-check-nodeport: "true"
349	  enable-health-check-loadbalancer-ip: "false"
350	  node-port-bind-protection: "true"
351	  enable-auto-protect-node-port-range: "true"
352	  bpf-lb-acceleration: "disabled"
353	  enable-service-topology: "{{ .EnableServiceTopology }}"
354	
355	  enable-svc-source-range-check: "true"
356	  enable-l2-neigh-discovery: "false"
357	  k8s-require-ipv4-pod-cidr: "false"
358	  k8s-require-ipv6-pod-cidr: "false"
359	  enable-k8s-networkpolicy: "true"
360	  enable-endpoint-lockdown-on-policy-overflow: "false"
361	  # Tell the agent to generate and write a CNI configuration file
362	  write-cni-conf-when-ready: /host/etc/cni/net.d/05-cilium.conflist
363	  cni-exclusive: "{{ .CniExclusive }}"
364	  cni-log-file: "/var/run/cilium/cilium-cni.log"
365	  # Disable health checking, when chaining mode is not set to portmap or none
366	  enable-endpoint-health-checking: "{{ .EnableEndpointHealthChecking }}"
367	  enable-health-checking: "true"
368	  health-check-icmp-failure-threshold: "3"
369	  enable-well-known-identities: "false"
370	  enable-node-selector-labels: "false"
371	  synchronize-k8s-nodes: "true"
372	  operator-api-serve-addr: "{{- if IsIPv6Only -}}[::1]{{- else -}}127.0.0.1{{- end -}}:9234"
373	
374	  {{ if WithDefaultBool .Hubble.Enabled false }}
375	  enable-hubble: "true"
376	  # UNIX domain socket for Hubble server to listen to.
377	  hubble-socket-path: "/var/run/cilium/hubble.sock"
378	  {{ if .Hubble.Metrics }}
379	  # Address to expose Hubble metrics (e.g. ":7070"). Metrics server will be disabled if this

enable-ipv4-masquerade: "{{ .Masquerade }}"
  enable-ipv4-big-tcp: "false"
  enable-ipv6-big-tcp: "false"
  enable-ipv6-masquerade: "false"
  enable-tcx: "true"
  datapath-mode: "veth"
  enable-bpf-masquerade: "{{ and (WithDefaultBool .EnableBPFMasquerade false) (not IsIPv6Only) }}"
  enable-masquerade-to-route-source: "false"
  {{ if .EnableEncryption }}
  {{ if eq .EncryptionType "ipsec" }}
  enable-ipsec: "true"
  ipsec-key-file: /etc/ipsec/keys
  enable-ipsec-key-watcher: "true"
  ipsec-key-rotation-duration: "5m"
  enable-ipsec-encrypted-overlay: "false"
  {{ else if eq .EncryptionType "wireguard"  }}
  enable-wireguard: "true"
  {{ end }}
  encrypt-node: "{{ .NodeEncryption }}"
  {{ end }}

  enable-xt-socket-fallback: "true"
  install-no-conntrack-iptables-rules: "false"
  iptables-random-fully: "false"

  auto-direct-node-routes: "{{ .AutoDirectNodeRoutes }}"
  direct-routing-skip-unreachable: "false"


  enable-local-redirect-policy: "{{ .EnableLocalRedirectPolicy }}"

  kube-proxy-replacement: "{{- if .EnableNodePort -}}true{{- else -}}false{{- end -}}"
  bpf-lb-sock: "false"
  enable-node-port: "{{ .EnableNodePort }}"
  nodeport-addresses: ""
  enable-health-check-nodeport: "true"
  enable-health-check-loadbalancer-ip: "false"
  node-port-bind-protection: "true"
  enable-auto-protect-node-port-range: "true"
  bpf-lb-acceleration: "disabled"
  enable-service-topology: "{{ .EnableServiceTopology }}"

  enable-svc-source-range-check: "true"
  enable-l2-neigh-discovery: "false"
  k8s-require-ipv4-pod-cidr: "false"
  k8s-require-ipv6-pod-cidr: "false"
  enable-k8s-networkpolicy: "true"
  enable-endpoint-lockdown-on-policy-overflow: "false"
  # Tell the agent to generate and write a CNI configuration file
  write-cni-conf-when-ready: /host/etc/cni/net.d/05-cilium.conflist
  cni-exclusive: "{{ .CniExclusive }}"
  cni-log-file: "/var/run/cilium/cilium-cni.log"
  # Disable health checking, when chaining mode is not set to portmap or none
  enable-endpoint-health-checking: "{{ .EnableEndpointHealthChecking }}"
  enable-health-checking: "true"
  health-check-icmp-failure-threshold: "3"
  enable-well-known-identities: "false"
  enable-node-selector-labels: "false"
  synchronize-k8s-nodes: "true"
  operator-api-serve-addr: "{{- if IsIPv6Only -}}[::1]{{- else -}}127.0.0.1{{- end -}}:9234"

  {{ if WithDefaultBool .Hubble.Enabled false }}
  enable-hubble: "true"
  # UNIX domain socket for Hubble server to listen to.
  hubble-socket-path: "/var/run/cilium/hubble.sock"
  {{ if .Hubble.Metrics }}
  # Address to expose Hubble metrics (e.g. ":7070"). Metrics server will be disabled if this
  # field is not set.
  hubble-metrics-server: ":9965"
  hubble-metrics-server-enable-tls: "false"
  enable-hubble-open-metrics: "false"
  # A space separated list of metrics to enable. See [0] for available metrics.
  #
  # https://github.com/cilium/hubble/blob/master/Documentation/metrics.md
  hubble-metrics:
  {{- range .Hubble.Metrics }}
    {{ . }}
  {{- end }}
  {{ end }}
  hubble-network-policy-correlation-enabled: "true"
  # An additional address for Hubble server to listen to (e.g. ":4244").
  hubble-listen-address: ":4244"
  hubble-disable-tls: "false"
  hubble-tls-cert-file: /var/lib/cilium/tls/hubble/server.crt
  hubble-tls-key-file: /var/lib/cilium/tls/hubble/server.key
  hubble-tls-client-ca-files: /var/lib/cilium/tls/hubble/client-ca.crt
  {{ end }}

  {{ with .IPAM }}
  ipam: {{ . }}
  ipam-cilium-node-update-rate: "15s"
  #cluster-pool-ipv4-cidr: "10.0.0.0/8"
  cluster-pool-ipv4-mask-size: "24"
  {{ if eq . "eni" }}
  enable-endpoint-routes: "true"
  auto-create-cilium-node-resource: "true"
  eni-tags: "{{ CloudLabels }}"
  {{ end }}
  {{ end }}

  default-lb-service-ipam: "lbipam"
  egress-gateway-reconciliation-trigger-interval: "1s"
  enable-vtep: "false"
  vtep-endpoint: ""
  vtep-cidr: ""
  vtep-mask: ""
  vtep-mac: ""
  procfs: "/host/proc"
  bpf-root: "/sys/fs/bpf"
  cgroup-root: "/run/cilium/cgroupv2"

  identity-management-mode: "agent"
  enable-sctp: "false"
  remove-cilium-node-taints: "true"
  set-cilium-node-taints: "true"
  set-cilium-is-up-condition: "true"
  unmanaged-pod-watcher-interval: "15"
  {{ if not .ChainingMode }}
  # default DNS proxy to transparent mode in non-chaining modes
  dnsproxy-enable-transparent-mode: "true"
  {{ end }}
  dnsproxy-socket-linger-timeout: "10"
  tofqdns-dns-reject-response-code: "{{ .ToFQDNsDNSRejectResponseCode }}"
  tofqdns-enable-dns-compression: "true"
  tofqdns-endpoint-max-ip-per-hostname: "1000"
  tofqdns-idle-connection-grace-period: "0s"
  tofqdns-max-deferred-connection-deletes: "10000"
  tofqdns-proxy-response-max-delay: "100ms"
  tofqdns-preallocate-identities:  "true"
  agent-not-ready-taint-key: "node.cilium.io/agent-not-ready"

  mesh-auth-enabled: "true"
  mesh-auth-queue-size: "1024"
  mesh-auth-rotated-identities-queue-size: "1024"
  mesh-auth-gc-interval: "5m0s"

  proxy-xff-num-trusted-hops-ingress: "0"
  proxy-xff-num-trusted-hops-egress: "0"
  proxy-connect-timeout: "2"
  proxy-initial-fetch-timeout: "30"
  proxy-max-requests-per-connection: "0"
  proxy-max-connection-duration-seconds: "0"
  proxy-idle-timeout-seconds: "60"
  proxy-max-concurrent-retries: "128"
  http-retry-count: "3"
  http-stream-idle-timeout: "300"

  external-envoy-proxy: "false"
  envoy-base-id: "0"
  envoy-access-log-buffer-size: "4096"
  envoy-keep-cap-netbindservice: "false"
  max-connected-clusters: "255"
  clustermesh-enable-endpoint-sync: "false"
  clustermesh-enable-mcs-api: "false"
  policy-default-local-cluster: "false"

  nat-map-stats-entries: "32"
  nat-map-stats-interval: "30s"
  enable-internal-traffic-policy: "true"
  enable-lb-ipam: "true"
  enable-non-default-deny-policies: "true"
  enable-source-ip-verification: "true"

# Extra config allows adding arbitrary properties to the cilium config.

{{ end }}

# Source: cilium/templates/cilium-agent/daemonset.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: cilium
  namespace: kube-system
  labels:
    k8s-app: cilium
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: cilium
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cilium-agent
    kubernetes.io/cluster-service: "true"
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      k8s-app: cilium
      kubernetes.io/cluster-service: "true"
  updateStrategy:
    type: OnDelete
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        {{ if .EnablePrometheusMetrics }}
        prometheus.io/port: "{{ .AgentPrometheusPort }}"
        prometheus.io/scrape: "true"
        {{ end }}
        # Set app AppArmor's profile to "unconfined". The value of this annotation
        # can be modified as long users know which profiles they have available
        # in AppArmor.
        container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/cilium-agent: "unconfined"
        container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/clean-cilium-state: "unconfined"
        container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/mount-cgroup: "unconfined"
        container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/apply-sysctl-overwrites: "unconfined"
        kubectl.kubernetes.io/default-container: cilium-agent
        {{- range $key, $value := .AgentPodAnnotations }}
        {{ $key }}: "{{ $value }}"
        {{- end }}
      labels:
        k8s-app: cilium
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cilium-agent
        app.kubernetes.io/part-of: cilium
        kubernetes.io/cluster-service: "true"
    spec:
      securityContext:
        seccompProfile:
          type: Unconfined
      containers:
      - name: cilium-agent
        image: "{{ or .Registry "quay.io" }}/cilium/cilium:{{ .Version }}"
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        command:
        - cilium-agent
        args:
        - --config-dir=/tmp/cilium/config-map
        startupProbe:
          httpGet:
            host: "{{- if IsIPv6Only -}}::1{{- else -}}127.0.0.1{{- end -}}"
            path: /healthz
            port: 9879
            scheme: HTTP
            httpHeaders:
            - name: "brief"
              value: "true"
          failureThreshold: 300
          periodSeconds: 2
          successThreshold: 1
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            host: "{{- if IsIPv6Only -}}::1{{- else -}}127.0.0.1{{- end -}}"
            path: /healthz
            port: 9879
            scheme: HTTP
            httpHeaders:
            - name: "brief"
              value: "true"
            - name: "require-k8s-connectivity"
              value: "false"
          periodSeconds: 30
          successThreshold: 1
          failureThreshold: 10
          timeoutSeconds: 5
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: {{ or .CPURequest "25m" }}
            memory: {{ or .MemoryRequest "128Mi" }}
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            host: "{{- if IsIPv6Only -}}::1{{- else -}}127.0.0.1{{- end -}}"
            path: /healthz
            port: 9879
            scheme: HTTP
            httpHeaders:
            - name: "brief"
              value: "true"
          periodSeconds: 30
          successThreshold: 1
          failureThreshold: 3
          timeoutSeconds: 5
        env:
        - name: K8S_NODE_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: spec.nodeName
        - name: CILIUM_K8S_NAMESPACE
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.namespace
        - name: CILIUM_CLUSTERMESH_CONFIG
          value: /var/lib/cilium/clustermesh/
        - name: GOMEMLIMIT
          valueFrom:
            resourceFieldRef:
              resource: limits.memory
              divisor: '1'
        - name: KUBE_CLIENT_BACKOFF_BASE
          value: "1"
        - name: KUBE_CLIENT_BACKOFF_DURATION
          value: "120"
        - name: KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST
          value: "{{ APIInternalName }}"
        - name: KUBERNETES_SERVICE_PORT
          value: "443"
        lifecycle:
          {{ if eq .IPAM "eni" }}
          postStart:
            exec:
              command:
              - "bash"
              - "-c"
              - |
                    set -o errexit
                    set -o pipefail
                    set -o nounset
                    
                    # When running in AWS ENI mode, it's likely that 'aws-node' has
                    # had a chance to install SNAT iptables rules. These can result
                    # in dropped traffic, so we should attempt to remove them.
                    # We do it using a 'postStart' hook since this may need to run
                    # for nodes which might have already been init'ed but may still
                    # have dangling rules. This is safe because there are no
                    # dependencies on anything that is part of the startup script
                    # itself, and can be safely run multiple times per node (e.g. in
                    # case of a restart).
                    if [[ "$(iptables-save | grep -E -c 'AWS-SNAT-CHAIN|AWS-CONNMARK-CHAIN')" != "0" ]];
                    then
                        echo 'Deleting iptables rules created by the AWS CNI VPC plugin'
                        iptables-save | grep -E -v 'AWS-SNAT-CHAIN|AWS-CONNMARK-CHAIN' | iptables-restore
                    fi
                    echo 'Done!'
          {{- end }}
          preStop:
            exec:
              command:
              - /cni-uninstall.sh
        ports:
        {{- if WithDefaultBool .Hubble.Enabled false }}
        - name: peer-service
          containerPort: 4244
          hostPort: 4244
          protocol: TCP
        {{- if .Hubble.Metrics }}
        - name: hubble-metrics
          containerPort: 9965
          hostPort: 9965
          protocol: TCP
        {{- end }}
        {{- end }}
        {{ if .EnablePrometheusMetrics }}
        - containerPort: {{ .AgentPrometheusPort }}
          name: prometheus
          protocol: TCP
        {{- end }}
        securityContext:
          seLinuxOptions:
            level: s0
            type: spc_t
          capabilities:
            add:
              - CHOWN
              - KILL
              - NET_ADMIN
              - NET_RAW
              - IPC_LOCK
              - SYS_MODULE
              - SYS_ADMIN
              - SYS_RESOURCE
              - DAC_OVERRIDE
              - FOWNER
              - SETGID
              - SETUID
            drop:
              - ALL
        terminationMessagePolicy: FallbackToLogsOnError
        volumeMounts:
        # Unprivileged containers need to mount /proc/sys/net from the host
        # to have write access
        - mountPath: /host/proc/sys/net
          name: host-proc-sys-net
        # Unprivileged containers need to mount /proc/sys/kernel from the host
        # to have write access
        - mountPath: /host/proc/sys/kernel
          name: host-proc-sys-kernel
        - name: bpf-maps
          mountPath: /sys/fs/bpf
          # Unprivileged containers can't set mount propagation to bidirectional
          # in this case we will mount the bpf fs from an init container that
          # is privileged and set the mount propagation from host to container
          # in Cilium.
          mountPropagation: HostToContainer
        - name: cilium-cgroup
          mountPath: /run/cilium/cgroupv2
        - name: cilium-run
          mountPath: /var/run/cilium
        - name: cilium-netns
          mountPath: /var/run/cilium/netns
          mountPropagation: HostToContainer
        - name: etc-cni-netd
          mountPath: /host/etc/cni/net.d
{{ if .EtcdManaged }}
        - name: etcd-config-path
          mountPath: /var/lib/etcd-config
          readOnly: true
        - name: etcd-secrets
          mountPath: /var/lib/etcd-secrets
          readOnly: true
{{ end }}
        - name: clustermesh-secrets
          mountPath: /var/lib/cilium/clustermesh
          readOnly: true
          # Needed to be able to load kernel modules
        - name: lib-modules
          mountPath: /lib/modules
          readOnly: true
        - name: xtables-lock
          mountPath: /run/xtables.lock
{{ if CiliumSecret }}
        - name: cilium-ipsec-secrets
          mountPath: /etc/ipsec
{{ end }}
{{ if WithDefaultBool .Hubble.Enabled false }}
        - name: hubble-tls
          mountPath: /var/lib/cilium/tls/hubble
          readOnly: true
{{ end }}
        - name: tmp
          mountPath: /tmp

{{ if .Debug }}
      - name: cilium-monitor
        image: "{{ or .Registry "quay.io" }}/cilium/cilium:{{ .Version }}"
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - --
        args:
        - |-
          for i in {1..5}; do \

content.content:
351	type CiliumNetworkingSpec struct {
352		// Registry overrides the default Cilium container registry (quay.io)
353		Registry string `json:"registry,omitempty"`
354	
355		// Version is the version of the Cilium agent and the Cilium Operator.
356		Version string `json:"version,omitempty"`
357	
358		// MemoryRequest memory request of Cilium agent + operator container. (default: 128Mi)
359		MemoryRequest *resource.Quantity `json:"memoryRequest,omitempty"`
360		// CPURequest CPU request of Cilium agent + operator container. (default: 25m)
361		CPURequest *resource.Quantity `json:"cpuRequest,omitempty"`
362	
363		// AgentPrometheusPort is the port to listen to for Prometheus metrics.
364		// Defaults to 9090.
365		AgentPrometheusPort int `json:"agentPrometheusPort,omitempty"`
366		// Metrics is a list of metrics to add or remove from the default list of metrics the agent exposes.
367		Metrics []string `json:"metrics,omitempty"`
368	
369		// ChainingMode allows using Cilium in combination with other CNI plugins.
370		// With Cilium CNI chaining, the base network connectivity and IP address management is managed
371		// by the non-Cilium CNI plugin, but Cilium attaches eBPF programs to the network devices created
372		// by the non-Cilium plugin to provide L3/L4 network visibility, policy enforcement and other advanced features.
373		// Default: none
374		ChainingMode string `json:"chainingMode,omitempty"`
375		// Debug runs Cilium in debug mode.
376		Debug bool `json:"debug,omitempty"`
377		// DisableEndpointCRD disables usage of CiliumEndpoint CRD.
378		// Default: false
379		DisableEndpointCRD bool `json:"disableEndpointCRD,omitempty"`
380		// EnablePolicy specifies the policy enforcement mode.
381		// "default": Follows Kubernetes policy enforcement.
382		// "always": Cilium restricts all traffic if no policy is in place.
383		// "never": Cilium allows all traffic regardless of policies in place.
384		// If unspecified, "default" policy mode will be used.
385		EnablePolicy string `json:"enablePolicy,omitempty"`
386		// EnableL7Proxy enables L7 proxy for L7 policy enforcement.
387		// Default: true
388		EnableL7Proxy *bool `json:"enableL7Proxy,omitempty"`
389		// EnableLocalRedirectPolicy that enables pod traffic destined to an IP address and port/protocol
390		// tuple or Kubernetes service to be redirected locally to backend pod(s) within a node, using eBPF.
391		// https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/network/kubernetes/local-redirect-policy/
392		// Default: false
393		EnableLocalRedirectPolicy *bool `json:"enableLocalRedirectPolicy,omitempty"`
394		// EnableBPFMasquerade enables masquerading packets from endpoints leaving the host with BPF instead of iptables.
395		// Default: false
396		EnableBPFMasquerade *bool `json:"enableBPFMasquerade,omitempty"`
397		// EnableEndpointHealthChecking enables connectivity health checking between virtual endpoints.
398		// Default: true
399		EnableEndpointHealthChecking *bool `json:"enableEndpointHealthChecking,omitempty"`
400		// EnablePrometheusMetrics enables the Cilium "/metrics" endpoint for both the agent and the operator.
401		EnablePrometheusMetrics bool `json:"enablePrometheusMetrics,omitempty"`
402		// EnableEncryption enables Cilium Encryption.
403		// Default: false
404		EnableEncryption bool `json:"enableEncryption,omitempty"`
405		// EncryptionType specifies Cilium Encryption method ("ipsec", "wireguard").
406		// Default: ipsec
407		EncryptionType CiliumEncryptionType `json:"encryptionType,omitempty"`
408		// NodeEncryption enables encryption for pure node to node traffic.
409		// Default: false
410		NodeEncryption bool `json:"nodeEncryption,omitempty"`
411		// IdentityAllocationMode specifies in which backend identities are stored ("crd", "kvstore").
412		// Default: crd
413		IdentityAllocationMode string `json:"identityAllocationMode,omitempty"`
414		// IdentityChangeGracePeriod specifies the duration to wait before using a changed identity.
415		// Default: 5s
416		IdentityChangeGracePeriod string `json:"identityChangeGracePeriod,omitempty"`
417		// Masquerade enables masquerading IPv4 traffic to external destinations behind the node IP.
418		// Default: false if IPAM is "eni" or in IPv6 mode, otherwise true
419		Masquerade *bool `json:"masquerade,omitempty"`
420		// AgentPodAnnotations makes possible to add additional annotations to cilium agent.
421		// Default: none
422		AgentPodAnnotations map[string]string `json:"agentPodAnnotations,omitempty"`
423		// OperatorPodAnnotations makes possible to add additional annotations to cilium operator.
424		// Default: none
425		OperatorPodAnnotations map[string]string `json:"operatorPodAnnotations,omitempty"`
426		// Tunnel specifies the Cilium tunnelling mode. Possible values are "vxlan", "geneve", or "disabled".
427		// Default: vxlan
428		Tunnel string `json:"tunnel,omitempty"`
429		// MonitorAggregation sets the level of packet monitoring. Possible values are "low", "medium", or "maximum".
430		// Default: medium
431		MonitorAggregation string `json:"monitorAggregation,omitempty"`
432		// BPFCTGlobalTCPMax is the maximum number of entries in the TCP CT table.
433		// Default: 524288
434		BPFCTGlobalTCPMax int `json:"bpfCTGlobalTCPMax,omitempty"`
435		// BPFCTGlobalAnyMax is the maximum number of entries in the non-TCP CT table.
436		// Default: 262144
437		BPFCTGlobalAnyMax int `json:"bpfCTGlobalAnyMax,omitempty"`
438		// BPFLBAlgorithm is the load balancing algorithm ("random", "maglev").
439		// Default: random
440		BPFLBAlgorithm string `json:"bpfLBAlgorithm,omitempty"`
441		// BPFLBMaglevTableSize is the per service backend table size when going with Maglev (parameter M).
442		// Default: 16381
443		BPFLBMaglevTableSize string `json:"bpfLBMaglevTableSize,omitempty"`
444		// BPFNATGlobalMax is the the maximum number of entries in the BPF NAT table.
445		// Default: 524288
446		BPFNATGlobalMax int `json:"bpfNATGlobalMax,omitempty"`
447		// BPFNeighGlobalMax is the the maximum number of entries in the BPF Neighbor table.
448		// Default: 524288
449		BPFNeighGlobalMax int `json:"bpfNeighGlobalMax,omitempty"`
450		// BPFPolicyMapMax is the maximum number of entries in endpoint policy map.
451		// Default: 16384
452		BPFPolicyMapMax int `json:"bpfPolicyMapMax,omitempty"`
453		// BPFLBMapMax is the maximum number of entries in bpf lb service, backend and affinity maps.
454		// Default: 65536
455		BPFLBMapMax int `json:"bpfLBMapMax,omitempty"`
456		// BPFLBSockHostNSOnly enables skipping socket LB for services when inside a pod namespace,
457		// in favor of service LB at the pod interface. Socket LB is still used when in the host namespace.
458		// Required by service mesh (e.g., Istio, Linkerd).
459		// Default: false
460		BPFLBSockHostNSOnly bool `json:"bpfLBSockHostNSOnly,omitempty"`
461		// PreallocateBPFMaps reduces the per-packet latency at the expense of up-front memory allocation.
462		// Default: true
463		PreallocateBPFMaps bool `json:"preallocateBPFMaps,omitempty"`
464		// SidecarIstioProxyImage is the regular expression matching compatible Istio sidecar istio-proxy
465		// container image names.
466		// Default: cilium/istio_proxy
467		SidecarIstioProxyImage string `json:"sidecarIstioProxyImage,omitempty"`
468		// ClusterName is the name of the cluster. It is only relevant when building a mesh of clusters.
469		ClusterName string `json:"clusterName,omitempty"`
470		// ClusterID is the ID of the cluster. It is only relevant when building a mesh of clusters.
471		// Must be a number between 1 and 255.
472		ClusterID uint8 `json:"clusterID,omitempty"`
473		// ToFQDNsDNSRejectResponseCode sets the DNS response code for rejecting DNS requests.
474		// Possible values are "nameError" or "refused".
475		// Default: refused
476		ToFQDNsDNSRejectResponseCode string `json:"toFQDNsDNSRejectResponseCode,omitempty"`
477		// ToFQDNsEnablePoller replaces the DNS proxy-based implementation of FQDN policies
478		// with the less powerful legacy implementation.
479		// Default: false
480		ToFQDNsEnablePoller bool `json:"toFQDNsEnablePoller,omitempty"`
481		// IPAM specifies the IP address allocation mode to use.
482		// Possible values are "crd" and "eni".
483		// "eni" will use AWS native networking for pods. Eni requires masquerade to be set to false.
484		// "crd" will use CRDs for controlling IP address management.
485		// "hostscope" will use hostscope IPAM mode.
486		// "kubernetes" will use addersing based on node pod CIDR.
487		// Default: "kubernetes".
488		IPAM string `json:"ipam,omitempty"`
489		// InstallIptablesRules enables installing the base IPTables rules used for masquerading and kube-proxy.
490		// Default: true
491		InstallIptablesRules *bool `json:"installIptablesRules,omitempty"`
492		// AutoDirectNodeRoutes adds automatic L2 routing between nodes.
493		// Default: false
494		AutoDirectNodeRoutes bool `json:"autoDirectNodeRoutes,omitempty"`
495		// EnableHostReachableServices configures Cilium to enable services to be
496		// reached from the host namespace in addition to pod namespaces.
497		// https://docs.cilium.io/en/v1.9/gettingstarted/host-services/
498		// Default: false
499		EnableHostReachableServices bool `json:"enableHostReachableServices,omitempty"`
500		// EnableNodePort replaces kube-proxy with Cilium's BPF implementation.
501		// Requires spec.kubeProxy.enabled be set to false.
502		// Default: false
503		EnableNodePort bool `json:"enableNodePort,omitempty"`
504		// EtcdManagd installs an additional etcd cluster that is used for Cilium state change.
505		// The cluster is operated by cilium-etcd-operator.
506		// Default: false
507		EtcdManaged bool `json:"etcdManaged,omitempty"`
508		// EnableRemoteNodeIdentity enables the remote-node-identity.
509		// Default: true
510		EnableRemoteNodeIdentity *bool `json:"enableRemoteNodeIdentity,omitempty"`
511		// EnableUnreachableRoutes enables unreachable routes on pod deletion.
512		// Default: false
513		EnableUnreachableRoutes *bool `json:"enableUnreachableRoutes,omitempty"`
514		// CniExclusive configures whether to remove other CNI configuration files.
515		// Default: true
516		CniExclusive *bool `json:"cniExclusive,omitempty"`
517		// Hubble configures the Hubble service on the Cilium agent.
518		Hubble *HubbleSpec `json:"hubble,omitempty"`
519	
520		// DisableCNPStatusUpdates determines if CNP NodeStatus updates will be sent to the Kubernetes api-server.
521		DisableCNPStatusUpdates *bool `json:"disableCNPStatusUpdates,omitempty"`
522	
523		// EnableServiceTopology determine if cilium should use topology aware hints.
524		EnableServiceTopology bool `json:"enableServiceTopology,omitempty"`
525	
526		// Ingress specifies the configuration for Cilium Ingress settings.
527		Ingress *CiliumIngressSpec `json:"ingress,omitempty"`
528	
529		// GatewayAPI specifies the configuration for Cilium Gateway API settings.
530		GatewayAPI *CiliumGatewayAPISpec `json:"gatewayAPI,omitempty"`
531	}
532	
533	// CiliumIngressSpec configures Cilium Ingress settings.
534	type CiliumIngressSpec struct {
535		// Enabled specifies whether Cilium Ingress is enabled.
536		Enabled *bool `json:"enabled,omitempty"`
537	
538		// EnforceHttps specifies whether HTTPS enforcement is enabled for Ingress traffic.
539		// Default: true
540		EnforceHttps *bool `json:"enforceHttps,omitempty"`
541	
542		// EnableSecretsSync specifies whether synchronization of secrets is enabled.
543		// Default: true
544		EnableSecretsSync *bool `json:"enableSecretsSync,omitempty"`
545	
546		// LoadBalancerAnnotationPrefixes specifies annotation prefixes for Load Balancer configuration.
547		// Default: "service.beta.kubernetes.io service.kubernetes.io cloud.google.com"
548		LoadBalancerAnnotationPrefixes string `json:"loadBalancerAnnotationPrefixes,omitempty"`
549	
550		// DefaultLoadBalancerMode specifies the default load balancer mode.
551		// Possible values: 'shared' or 'dedicated'
552		// Default: dedicated
553		DefaultLoadBalancerMode string `json:"defaultLoadBalancerMode,omitempty"`
554	
555		// SharedLoadBalancerServiceName specifies the name of the shared load balancer service.
556		// Default: cilium-ingress
557		SharedLoadBalancerServiceName string `json:"sharedLoadBalancerServiceName,omitempty"`
558	}
559	
560	// CiliumGatewayAPISpec configures Cilium Gateway API settings.
561	type CiliumGatewayAPISpec struct {
562		// Enabled specifies whether Cilium Gateway API is enabled.
563		Enabled *bool `json:"enabled,omitempty"`
564	
565		// EnableSecretsSync specifies whether synchronization of secrets is enabled.
566		// Default: true
567		EnableSecretsSync *bool `json:"enableSecretsSync,omitempty"`
568	}
569	
570	// HubbleSpec configures the Hubble service on the Cilium agent.
571	type HubbleSpec struct {
572		// Enabled decides if Hubble is enabled on the agent or not
573		Enabled *bool `json:"enabled,omitempty"`
574	
575		// Metrics is a list of metrics to collect. If empty or null, metrics are disabled.
576		// See https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/observability/metrics/#hubble-exported-metrics
577		Metrics []string `json:"metrics,omitempty"`
578	}

582	type LyftVPCNetworkingSpec struct {
583		SubnetTags map[string]string `json:"subnetTags,omitempty"`
584	}

604		// node agent masquerading rules

608	}

42	func TestBootstrapChannelBuilder_BuildTasks(t *testing.T) {
43		h := testutils.NewIntegrationTestHarness(t)

45	
46		h.SetupMockAWS()
47	
48		runChannelBuilderTest(t, "simple", []string{"kops-controller.addons.k8s.io-k8s-1.16"})
49		// Use cilium networking, proxy
50		runChannelBuilderTest(t, "cilium", []string{"kops-controller.addons.k8s.io-k8s-1.16"})
51		runChannelBuilderTest(t, "amazonvpc", []string{"networking.amazon-vpc-routed-eni-k8s-1.16"})

83	func runChannelBuilderTest(t *testing.T, key string, addonManifests []string) {
84		ctx := context.TODO()
85	
86		basedir := path.Join("tests/bootstrapchannelbuilder/", key)
87	
88		clusterYamlPath := path.Join(basedir, "cluster.yaml")
89		clusterYaml, err := os.ReadFile(clusterYamlPath)
90		if err != nil {
91			t.Fatalf("error reading cluster yaml file %q: %v", clusterYamlPath, err)
92		}
93		obj, _, err := kopscodecs.Decode(clusterYaml, nil)
94		if err != nil {
95			t.Fatalf("error parsing cluster yaml %q: %v", clusterYamlPath, err)
96		}
97		cluster := obj.(*kopsapi.Cluster)
98	
99		cloud, err := BuildCloud(cluster)
100		if err != nil {
101			t.Fatalf("error from BuildCloud: %v", err)
102		}
103	
104		if err := PerformAssignments(cluster, vfs.Context, cloud); err != nil {
105			t.Fatalf("error from PerformAssignments for %q: %v", key, err)
106		}
107	
108		fullSpec, err := mockedPopulateClusterSpec(ctx, cluster, cloud)
109		if err != nil {
110			t.Fatalf("error from PopulateClusterSpec for %q: %v", key, err)
111		}
112		cluster = fullSpec
113	
114		templates, err := templates.LoadTemplates(ctx, cluster, models.NewAssetPath("cloudup/resources"))
115		if err != nil {
116			t.Fatalf("error building templates for %q: %v", key, err)
117		}
118	
119		vfs.Context.ResetMemfsContext(true)
120	
121		basePath, err := vfs.Context.BuildVfsPath("memfs://tests")
122		if err != nil {
123			t.Errorf("error building vfspath: %v", err)
124		}
125		clientset := vfsclientset.NewVFSClientset(vfs.Context, basePath)
126	
127		secretStore, err := clientset.SecretStore(cluster)
128		if err != nil {
129			t.Error(err)
130		}
131		role := "arn:aws:iam::1234567890108:instance-profile/kops-custom-node-role"
132		kopsModel := model.KopsModelContext{
133			IAMModelContext: iam.IAMModelContext{
134				Cluster:      cluster,
135				AWSAccountID: "123456789012",
136				AWSPartition: "aws-test",
137			},
138			Region: "us-east-1",
139			InstanceGroups: []*kopsapi.InstanceGroup{
140				{
141					Spec: kopsapi.InstanceGroupSpec{
142						IAM: &kopsapi.IAMProfileSpec{
143							Profile: &role,
144						},
145						Role: kopsapi.InstanceGroupRoleNode,
146					},
147				},
148				{
149					Spec: kopsapi.InstanceGroupSpec{
150						Role: kopsapi.InstanceGroupRoleNode,
151					},
152				},
153			},
154		}
155	
156		kopsModel.AllInstanceGroups = kopsModel.InstanceGroups
157	
158		tf := &TemplateFunctions{
159			KopsModelContext: kopsModel,
160			cloud:            cloud,
161		}
162		tf.AddTo(templates.TemplateFunctions, secretStore)
163	
164		bcb := bootstrapchannelbuilder.NewBootstrapChannelBuilder(
165			&kopsModel,
166			fi.LifecycleSync,
167			assets.NewAssetBuilder(vfs.Context, cluster.Spec.Assets, false),
168			templates,
169			nil,
170		)
171	
172		context := &fi.CloudupModelBuilderContext{
173			Tasks: make(map[string]fi.CloudupTask),
174		}
175		err = bcb.Build(context)
176		if err != nil {
177			t.Fatalf("error from BootstrapChannelBuilder Build: %v", err)
178		}
179	
180		{
181			name := cluster.ObjectMeta.Name + "-addons-bootstrap"
182			manifestTask := context.Tasks["ManagedFile/"+name]
183			if manifestTask == nil {
184				t.Fatalf("manifest task not found (%q)", name)
185			}
186	
187			manifestFileTask := manifestTask.(*fitasks.ManagedFile)
188			actualManifest, err := fi.ResourceAsString(manifestFileTask.Contents)
189			if err != nil {
190				t.Fatalf("error getting manifest as string: %v", err)
191			}
192	
193			expectedManifestPath := path.Join(basedir, "manifest.yaml")
194			golden.AssertMatchesFile(t, actualManifest, expectedManifestPath)
195		}
196	
197		for _, k := range addonManifests {
198			name := cluster.ObjectMeta.Name + "-addons-" + k
199			manifestTask := context.Tasks["ManagedFile/"+name]
200			if manifestTask == nil {
201				for k := range context.Tasks {
202					t.Logf("found task %s", k)
203				}
204				t.Fatalf("manifest task not found (%q)", name)
205			}
206	
207			manifestFileTask := manifestTask.(*fitasks.ManagedFile)
208			actualManifest, err := fi.ResourceAsString(manifestFileTask.Contents)
209			if err != nil {
210				t.Fatalf("error getting manifest as string: %v", err)
211			}
212	
213			expectedManifestPath := path.Join(basedir, k+".yaml")
214			golden.AssertMatchesFile(t, actualManifest, expectedManifestPath)
215		}
216	}

kind: Cluster
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2016-12-10T22:42:27Z"
  name: minimal.example.com
spec:
  addons:
    - manifest: s3://somebucket/example.yaml
  kubernetesApiAccess:
  - 0.0.0.0/0
  channel: stable
  cloudProvider: aws
  configBase: memfs://clusters.example.com/minimal.example.com

  - etcdMembers:
    - instanceGroup: master-us-test-1a

    name: us-test-1a
